paper_focus.title: Microstructural Modulation through Process Control Leading to Ultrastrong Ductile Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% Low-Density Steel
paper_focus.research_objective: To quantify the precipitation strengthening contribution of the B2 second phase, clarify the fracture behavior, and through systematic process parameter variation achieve delayed tensile failure to develop an ultrastrong ductile low-density steel.
main_process_variables: Hot rolling (1050 °C to 900 °C finish, 14 mm to 3 mm, 9 passes)
Cold rolling (room temperature, 3 mm to 2 mm)
Annealing at 900 °C for 5–30 min followed by water quenching
Tensile testing (ASTM E8, room temperature, gauge length 12.5 mm, crosshead speed 1 mm min⁻¹, rolling direction)
expected_information_axis: microstructure (phase fractions, B2 band morphology, recrystallization, precipitate size and distribution)
property (yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, elongation, toughness, strain hardening exponent)
mechanism (strain partitioning, local strain, GND density, crack nucleation)
